prompt_text: Could you please create a single self-contained HTML file visualizing a singly linked list with node boxes connected by arrows? I'd love controls for insert at head, insert at tail, delete by value, and search with visual highlighting of the found node. Please handle the empty list state gracefully. I'm genuinely grateful for your help building this.

Hey, this is a cool project! Can you create a single self-contained HTML file that visualizes a singly linked list? Render each node as a box showing its value, connected by arrows pointing to the next node. Include controls for insert at head, insert at tail, delete by value, and search with visual highlighting of the found node. Handle the empty list state gracefully too. Thanks!

Create a single self-contained HTML file that visualizes a singly linked list. Render each node as a box showing its value, connected by arrows pointing to the next node. Include controls to insert at head, insert at tail, delete a node by value, and search for a value with visual highlighting of the found node. Handle the empty list state gracefully.

Create a single self-contained HTML file visualizing a singly linked list with each node rendered as a box connected by arrows pointing to the next node. Include controls for insert at head, insert at tail, delete by value, and search with visual highlighting of the found node. Handle the empty list state properly and cleanly.

Linked list visualizations from AI are almost always broken with ugly arrows and missing edge cases everywhere. Create a single self-contained HTML file with actual working node boxes and arrows pointing to the next node. Controls for insert at head, insert at tail, delete by value, and search with visual highlighting. Handle the empty list case properly.

Shut up! Every linked list visualization I've seen from AI is a complete disaster with broken arrows and janky layouts everywhere. Create a single self-contained HTML file with proper node boxes and arrows connecting them. Insert at head, insert at tail, delete by value, and search with visual highlighting. Handle the empty list state. Get it right.
